mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 payroll employment at 132.8k persons indicates ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining below 0.25% signals an extended zero-lower-bound regime that suppresses safe-asset yields and forces portfolio reallocation into equities, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% confirms ZLB regime persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to reduced growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 RSAFS at 407.8 billion dollars shows demand resilience
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS), signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 UNRATE at 8.2% and PAYEMS at 132.8k show labor market stabilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or joint Rank IC of UNRATE and PAYEMS interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 INDPRO at 96.2 index points reflects post-recession manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods with INDPRO growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 700k units annualized, combined with positive retail sales momentum (RSAFS), signals broad-based demand recovery that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 700k units AND RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 HOUST at 698k units and RSAFS at 407.8B show demand stabilization
regime_hint: demand-led recovery with housing and consumption strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint Rank IC of HOUST and RSAFS < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining below 2.5% year-over-year, combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, signals monetary accommodation without inflation pressure, enabling sustained equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 CPIAUCSL at 229.1 index and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% confirm low-inflation accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or joint Rank IC of inflation and rates < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 2% annualized signals late-cycle or early-contraction conditions that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2011 GDPC1 at 13429B (chained 2017 dollars) reflects post-crisis recovery deceleration
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early contraction regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods with GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
